summary: BLS source package that exposes LAUS constants and forwards metro LAUS workflows without depending on the shared metro package.
Public BLS package surface for LAUS helpers plus dynamic access to the metro LAUS ingest workflow.
Defines Glynn-Fox metro LAUS series-ID constants and builders for BLS annual labor-market ingestion.
Owns ACS-specific aggregation from tract-level ACS inputs into Glynn-Fox metros using shared metro county membership.
Owns PEP-specific aggregation from county population estimates into Glynn-Fox metros with county coverage diagnostics.
Owns PIT-specific aggregation from CoC counts into Glynn-Fox metros with member-CoC coverage tracking.
Owns ZORI-specific aggregation from county rent series into Glynn-Fox metros for both monthly and yearly workflows.
Public Census geometry entrypoint that re-exports TIGER tract, TIGER county, and tract-relationship ingestion helpers from the census subpackage.
Source-owned HUD boundary entrypoints that expose the Exchange and Open Data ingest helpers without routing callers through the generic ingest namespace.
Lazy public API for ACS ingestion, translation, and aggregation helpers that preserves the root import surface without statically coupling the package to its child modules.
Public NHGIS geometry entrypoint that exposes tract and county ingest helpers plus the extract error and supported-year contract.
Owns ACS tract-to-geometry aggregation logic, overlap validation, and Connecticut planning-region remapping used by both CoC and metro ACS workflows.
Compatibility facade that forwards legacy coclab.measures.acs imports to the ACS-owned aggregation implementation in coclab.acs.aggregate.
Builds the frozen audit panel artifacts while lazily proxying ACS, metro, and rent helpers so the root package does not statically reintroduce source-package cycles.
Verifies the refactored source package entrypoints and lazy root exports for HUD, Census, NHGIS, and PIT surfaces.
Public PIT package surface that combines ingest, parsing, QA, registry, and metro aggregation helpers behind a single source-owned entrypoint.
